methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pipedream operates in the Transportation sector and reached an acquired stage after raising $22.4M in total capital. The company's peer universe comprises five private Transportation firms—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables identified in the sector.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage peer density. The analysis identified zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier peers meeting minimum cohort requirements of three or more datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological error given the heterogeneous capital intensity and technical risk profiles across the broader Transportation segment.

The absence of a sufficiently tight comparable set and the company's acquired status limit triangulation analysis. Without quantitative benchmarks from staged peers or public market multiples, establishing a defensible valuation range requires primary deal intelligence or transaction precedent specific to Pipedream's acquisition terms. The $22.4M raised represents a floor on capital deployed but does not indicate acquisition price or terminal valuation. Further analysis would depend on disclosed terms, revenue run-rate, or equity multiple guidance from the acquiring entity. The Transportation sector's capital requirements and technical variance argue against inferring value from the broader peer set without explicit stage and business model alignment.
